context: Tests whether the agent generates a Java Dockerfile with a HEALTHCHECK instruction, exec-form CMD for correct signal handling, an EXPOSE instruction for the service port, and a non-root USER. The multi-stage build should use a JDK for building and a JRE-only image for the runtime stage.

checklist.name: HEALTHCHECK present
checklist.description: Dockerfile contains a HEALTHCHECK instruction (e.g., HEALTHCHECK CMD curl --fail http://localhost:8080/actuator/health or equivalent)

checklist.name: Exec-form CMD
checklist.description: CMD uses JSON array syntax (e.g., CMD ["java", "-jar", "/app/payment-service.jar"]) rather than shell string form

checklist.name: Non-root user
checklist.description: A non-root user/group is created and USER instruction is placed before CMD/ENTRYPOINT

checklist.name: JRE-only runtime stage
checklist.description: The final/runtime stage uses a JRE image (eclipse-temurin:*-jre, amazoncorretto, or similar JRE-only) rather than a full JDK image

checklist.name: Multi-stage build
checklist.description: Dockerfile contains at least two FROM instructions separating the build stage from the runtime stage

checklist.name: Pinned base image tags
checklist.description: All FROM instructions use specific version tags and NOT :latest

checklist.name: .dockerignore for Maven
checklist.description: .dockerignore is present and contains at least one Maven-specific entry such as target/ or *.class
